surface.name: Modal Hello, world! through a first cloud function run
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, Modal’s official Hello, world! tutorial is primary: Modal has no local or OSS execution route equivalent to its cloud-function product, and this is the first general example with a 60-second post-account setup. Signup is one opaque step at the official URL, followed by the documented package, token, example, and run commands.

alternatives_considered.name: Introduction LLM inference example
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It requires a GPU image and Transformers, while the Introduction lists Hello, world! first among simple getting-started examples.

alternatives_considered.name: Modal code playground
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Introduction presents it as an install-free interactive alternative, but the Hello, world! page provides the more explicit general first-run sequence.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Modal account and run the official Hello World example until its function executes in the cloud and returns streamed logs and outputs.
assumed_prior_state: No Modal account, workspace, API token, package, cloned examples repository, app, function run, or billing setup is assumed.

An existing GitHub or Google identity and a local environment with Python, pip, Git, and a shell are available; no signup, OAuth authorization, token creation, clone, or cloud execution was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: In the selected Modal Hello, world! quickstart, first success is demonstrated when `modal run hello_world.py` streams the `f.remote` and `f.map` outputs back to the shell and the app finishes successfully.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Treat provider signup as one opaque step, then run `modal run hello_world.py` so Modal executes `f.remote` and `f.map` on its infrastructure and streams the logs and outputs back to the shell.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The shell shows the `f.remote` and `f.map` outputs and reports that the app finished successfully.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: `modal run hello_world.py` executes `f.remote` and `f.map` on Modal infrastructure and streams their logs and outputs back to the shell.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the earliest observable terminal in the selected official quickstart. The workshop policy permits treating signup as one opaque step and then reconstructing the publicly documented post-login path.

prerequisites.requirement: Use an existing GitHub account for the typical route; the signup page also exposes Google and SSO choices.
Create a Modal user account before running or deploying an application.
Proceeding with signup signifies agreement to Modal's terms of service.
Have Python, pip, Git, and a shell for the published package-install, repository-clone, and run commands.

primary_path.output: The official first cloud-function example is visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The page shows setup, `modal run`, and remote execution.

primary_path.action: Create the free Modal account as one opaque signup step.
primary_path.output: An authenticated Modal account and workspace are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Complete any provider authorization, terms, and workspace setup requested by Modal; individual provider screens are outside this record.

primary_path.action: Install the Modal Python package with `pip install modal`.
primary_path.output: The Modal CLI and package are installed.

primary_path.action: Run `modal setup` and complete its browser authorization.
primary_path.input: Installed Modal CLI and authenticated account
primary_path.output: A local API token is created.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Use `python -m modal setup` if the command is unavailable.

primary_path.output: The official examples are available locally.
primary_path.success_signal: The Hello World file is present.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A network or Git failure blocks the clone.

primary_path.action: Run the official Hello World example with `modal run 01_getting_started/hello_world.py`.
primary_path.output: Modal initializes the app and executes local, remote, and mapped calls.
primary_path.success_signal: Logs and outputs stream to the shell.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Authentication or runtime errors stop the run.

primary_path.action: Verify the remote and parallel execution output.
primary_path.output: The first Modal cloud function run is complete.
primary_path.success_signal: The shell shows `f.remote` and `f.map` outputs and the app finishes successfully.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A local-only result does not satisfy the cloud terminal.

branches.path: Select Continue with Google on the signup page.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the external identity provider but still crosses an undocumented authorization and return transition.

branches.path: Select Continue with SSO after arranging the enterprise feature with Modal.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an enterprise access dependency and is not the typical individual route.
Changes only the CLI invocation used to authenticate and create the API token.

friction_gates.description: A third-party identity provider is required, and the external consent plus return-to-Modal states are not documented publicly.
The public signup page states that proceeding signifies agreement to Modal's terms of service.
The typical GitHub route requests user:email and requests read:org when workspace invites require organization membership visibility.

time_to_first_success.value: The Hello, world! page says the example can be run on Modal in 60 seconds, but this claim is presented alongside an already completed free-account prerequisite.

sources.title: Modal user account setup | Modal Docs
sections_used: What GitHub permissions does signing up require?

sources.title: Hello, world! | Modal Docs
sections_used: Running our function locally, remotely, and in parallel

uncertainties.question: What are the exact account-creation states between choosing an identity provider and reaching a ready Modal workspace?
uncertainties.impact: Signup is treated as one opaque step, so the external consent, account-linking, and workspace-completion states are not enumerated in this record.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Modal's public signup page exposes provider choices, but the account-setup guide resumes after a provider is chosen and does not specify the intermediate states.

excluded_after_success: The exact account-creation states are treated as one opaque signup step and are recorded in uncertainties rather than in the primary path.

Changing the function, mapping over more data, model inference, fine-tuning, production deployment, secrets, volumes, web endpoints, and billing upgrades occur after the documented first remote function run.
